claims.evidence_text: In Nepal, approximately two-thirds of the total wheat growing area is under the pressure of spot blotch disease in January and February, where the mean temperature is 16-18o C, respectively, and the relative humidity (RH) is more than 90%
claims.claim_summary: Spot blotch (Bipolaris sorokiniana) pressure on wheat is high in January-February when mean temperature is 16-18 C and RH >90%

claims.evidence_text: the mean disease severity percentage of spot blotch for TS and LS wheat crops was 31.3% and 44.9%,respectively
claims.claim_summary: Spot blotch (Bipolaris sorokiniana) mean disease severity in timely-sown wheat was 31.3%

claims.evidence_text: the mean disease severity percentage of spot blotch for TS and LS wheat crops was 31.3% and 44.9%,respectively
claims.claim_summary: Spot blotch (Bipolaris sorokiniana) mean disease severity in late-sown wheat was 44.9% (higher than timely sowing)

claims.evidence_text: The disease results in substantial yield reductions, ranging from 22 percent to complete crop failure during severe outbreaks (Chowdhury et al., 2013)
claims.claim_summary: Spot blotch (Bipolaris sorokiniana) causes wheat yield reductions ranging from 22% to complete crop failure during severe outbreaks

claims.evidence_text: The late sowing date can increase the disease severity in the wheat crop, thereby significantly affecting yield and productivity.
claims.claim_summary: Late sowing of wheat increases spot blotch (Bipolaris sorokiniana) disease severity; timely sowing reduces it

claims.evidence_text: Timely sowing is important componentsofintegratedcropmanagementforspot blotch (Basnet et al., 2022).
claims.claim_summary: Timely sowing is an important integrated crop management component for managing wheat spot blotch (Bipolaris sorokiniana)
